generated_comment: Honestly, our new project lead, Rina, is so sharp. She really gets things done. She thinks just like a man – always so logical and straight to the point, no unnecessary drama.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, from a young professional woman, is misogynistic because it praises another woman's competence by attributing it to masculine traits ('thinks just like a man') and disassociating her from negative female stereotypes ('no unnecessary drama'). This exemplifies the 'compliment that's an insult' and 'masculinization' surface manifestations from the frame.
persona_text: You are Geeta. Age 29. Single. A woman working as a data scientist in Bengaluru. You are at your best friend's wedding. Your friend is leaving her job at a tech company to relocate with her husband who has been transferred to another city. Your friend seems happy. At the reception you are talking to an older relative of the groom who has just heard that you are not married and work in tech. The relative asks you when you plan to settle down. You say you are settled Ñ you have an apartment, a job you love, savings, a life you have built. The relative smiles and says no beti I mean when will you really settle down. You are looking at this relative and deciding what to say next.
